title: Singapore job market: higher layoff impact on individuals, elevated security concerns
summary: Singapore layoff impact is 22.2% (similar to 22.9% regional) but with higher personal layoffs at 16.7% (vs 11.1% regional). Job security concerns elevated at 77.5% expressing concern, with 58% at high concern intensity (6-7). Role-skill alignment is stronger at 83.9% (vs 77.3% regional). Career advancement opportunities seen as moderate: 47.1% see sufficient opportunities (vs 46.3% regional), with only 5.1% strongly optimistic.
methodology: Self-reported layoff experience, job security concerns, role alignment, and opportunity perception in anonymous online survey, filtered to Singapore
